summary:
Fixed 3 review gaps in the vendor statement reconciliation workflow:
1. Migration 068: Changed tenant_id from nullable to NOT NULL on both vendor_statements and vendor_statement_lines. Rewrote as CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS since no prior migration creates the base table.
2. get_lines() now filters by tenant_id, preventing cross-tenant data access. All 4 callers in route handler updated.
3. update_line() now validates that matched_invoice_id belongs to the same vendor and tenant via validate_invoice_ownership(), fetches the line to compute actual variance (line.amount_cents - invoice_amount) instead of always storing 0.

Backend builds cleanly. All 22 vendor statement tests pass.
decisions_made: Rewrote migration 068 as CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS instead of ALTER TABLE since the base vendor_statements table didn't exist in prior migrations
Made tenant_id NOT NULL on both tables to prevent cross-tenant data leakage
Added validate_invoice_ownership() and get_line() repo methods for proper invoice validation during manual matching
Changed StatementLineItem.tenant_id from Option<TenantId> to TenantId to reflect NOT NULL constraint
open_questions: Does the vendor_statements table actually exist in production from a prior deployment? If so, the migration needs to remain as ALTER TABLE with a NOT NULL constraint added after data cleanup.
next_steps: Run full DB integration tests with live PostgreSQL to verify the migration applies cleanly
Run web frontend build to verify TypeScript compilation
Add a manual match test case that verifies invoice ownership validation rejects cross-vendor invoices
